headline: Oil Surges Past $110 as Trump Threatens ‘Hell’ for Iran — Which Energy Stocks Could Catch the Rally?
summary: Crude oil prices have surged past $110 a barrel amid rising tensions in the Middle East. Over the weekend, President Donald Trump warned that etfs">Iran it could face attacks on bridges and power plants if the vital Strait of Hormuz is not reopened. As a result, Brent crude (CM:BZ) hovered just around $110, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) (CM:CL) neared $112. Here are three energy stocks — Schlumberger (SLB), Devon Energy (DVN), and Baker Hughes Company (BKR) — that analysts say have upside
